channel.title: Posts on tobeif-DTU
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on tobeif-DTU

item.title: Siemens Fonden supports DTU Remanufactory student
item.description: Fantastic appreciation from Siemens Fonden to one our Remanufactory BSc students. In his thesis, Mikkel has worked on an initial batch-disassembly prototype (incl. variant and feature recognition, tool changer, data extraction, etc.) to explore the technologies that will be at the heart of our rapid value recovery disassembly setup in our newly established Remanufactory lab. And we look more than forward to keeping Mikkel as part of that story as the grant will allow him to further develop his alpha prototype during his MSc studies.

item.title: Look for your twin @Roskilde
item.description:
Going to wonderful Roskilde this week? In this case, make sure to visit our Twinfinder group, just one example from our Design of Mechatronics class contributing to the Roskilde Festival - powered by DTU Students initiative.

In brief, the group has built an automatic Twin Finder system to spark spontaneous encounters, and perhaps ask some deeper questions regarding social networks, biases in AI, etc. Perhaps 😃)! But definitely great fun!

item.title: Configuration design
item.description:
Last week, our student Penelope successfully defended her MSc thesis presenting a wonderful example of a configuration design task, i.e., the task of rethinking/exploring the space of mechanical configurations based on design trade-off knowledge.

Quechua MH 500 Rooftop Tent on a picture from the MSc thesis (Penelope to the right)

Project: In close collaboration with Decathlon, Penelope applied this idea of configuration design on car rooftop tents providing valuable input to the design team and sparking new project ideas for potential future solutions. A very cool product and a fantastic case example to ask the question what we can actually achieve based o a systematic understanding of active constraints and trade-offs between the various design objectives (instead of simple design heuristics / best-practices).

item.title: Fantastic work on repurposing electronic waste, but ...
item.description:
Estonian researchers have repurposed 15-year-old smartphones as tiny underwater data centers, outperforming popular IoT-specific devices such as the Rasperry Pi (Link here). Without doubt a really nice and innovative idea for addressing and highlighting the electronic waste problem!

However, one of the main aspects gets easily lost in a lot of media coverage! The research team needed to use an old line of phones, discontinued in 2016, as almost all manufacturers systematically reduced modularity of their models, moved towards increasingly proprietary software, and implemented manufacturing approaches that make it impossible to remove/replace/repair components.

What are the next steps:

  • on the more applied side Remanufacturing (selective disassembly processes, embedded disassembly, Partially distructive disassembly planning, Geometry assurance, Virtual backbone)
  • on the digitalisation side: simulation-driven design, e.g., applied variation based simulation (incl. sampling and statistical modelling) & Digital thread in industry & MBSE, or also in its combination with data driven design as a Hybrid digital twin solution (Inverse modelling for non … in processes and combination with active learning loops)- enabling also useful for the link to self-driving labs, Bioscience & medical production,
    Last step is what we called Ideal Design / Trade-off based design
  • Reasons for work in this field
    • large gap between Design Methods & Detailed parametric representations
    • Role of Embodying a physical device / configuring different functionalitities is often unclear
    • addressing this Duality of Design (ambition of Top Down Design is clear, either supporting wide exploration of conceptual ideas and avoiding biases, or also supporting planning and organisation of design processes, incl. traceability in SE / at the same time, Design needs to be bottom up, i.e. considering detailed constraints)
  • Many design processes are co-design of elements and overall structure, in mechanics with numerous interdependecies & also changes to the conceptual solution
    • These design approaches have historically relied on intuition-based standard templates with a finite set of parameters
    • circuit design: co-design process essentially aims to tailor the electromagnetic structures and the active circuit topologies to optimize the collective response of the end-to-end circuit.
    • by choosing a template, we have already narrowed down the design and trade-off space.
    • quite possible that an entire design space that lies outside the scope of these regular template-based geometries can yield significant improvement over traditional solutions
    • for inverse design: The complex impedances of these devices, their ability to amplify, and theirmutual interactions need to be taken into account to realize an optimized endto-end design process.
  • Other direction Computational Exploration (asking the question of well-structured)
    • Complete exploration of design spaces is often computationally prohibitive
    • The role of the designer therefore shifts from the generation of solutions to the definition of design problems and the evaluation of solutions with design options generated and scored computationally (Biskjaer, Dalsgaard & Halskov 2014).
    • engineers use design exploration (often referred to as meta-heuristics) and optimisation algorithms that intelligently navigate the problem space to find optimal solutions.
